name: QR Code Phishing (Quishing) Analysis
description: Detects and responds to QR code phishing (quishing) attacks. Extracts URLs embedded in QR codes from email attachments or images, enriches the extracted URLs against threat intelligence, and makes a disposition decision. Malicious QR phishing emails are quarantined, the embedded URL is blocked on the web proxy, and the SOC is notified. Addresses the growing trend of attackers using QR codes to bypass traditional URL scanning.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: VirusTotal
required_integrations.reason: Check extracted QR code URL against threat intelligence

data.title: QR Phishing Alert
data.summary: Triggers on QR code phishing or quishing alerts

data.title: Extract QR Code URL
data.summary: Decode QR code from email attachment/image and extract the embedded URL

script.code:
import re

def main(alert):
    qr_data = alert.get('qr_decoded', alert.get('qr_url', ''))
    attachments = alert.get('attachments', [])
    extracted_urls = []
    if qr_data:
        url_pattern = r'https?://[^\s<>"]+'
        urls = re.findall(url_pattern, qr_data)
        extracted_urls.extend(urls)
    for att in attachments:
        if att.get('qr_decoded'):
            urls = re.findall(r'https?://[^\s<>"]+', att['qr_decoded'])
            extracted_urls.extend(urls)
    extracted_urls = list(set(extracted_urls))
    return {
        'urls': extracted_urls,
        'url_count': len(extracted_urls),
        'has_qr': len(extracted_urls) > 0,
        'primary_url': extracted_urls[0] if extracted_urls else '',
        'qr_image': attachments[0].get('filename', 'unknown') if attachments else 'inline'
    }
